title: Clinical Trial Liaison Manager
description:
Clinical Trial Liaison Manager


**ID:** 2525
**Date of Posting:** Apr 27, 2026
**Business Area:** Research \& Development
**Job Type:** Direct Employee
**On site / Remote / Hybrid:** Hybrid
**Hub Office:**Milano, IT
**Territory:****Full\-Time or Part\-Time:** Full Time
**Seniority:** Mid\-senior
*With its beginnings in a family run pharmacy in Correggio, Italy in the 1920s, Recordati is now a global pharmaceutical company, listed on the Italian stock exchange, with over 4,500 employees and turnover of over Euro 2bn.*  

  

*We are a group of like\-minded, passionate individuals who go to extraordinary lengths for our patients, customers, partners, investors and the people across the globe who we serve. We develop and commercialise medicines to serve people living with common diseases, as well as those living with some of the rarest, in around 150 countries.*  

  

*At Recordati, our mantra is simple. We’ve always believed that health, and the opportunity to live life to the fullest, is a right, not a privilege. Whether that is for common diseases or the rarest – we want to give people the opportunity to be the best version of themselves.*  

  

*This drive will never stop. Together, we will always be reimagining tomorrow – with new ideas, new technologies and new innovations to fight diseases.*  

  

*Recordati. Unlocking the full potential of life.*


**Job Purpose**

The Clinical Trial Liaison Manager (CTLM) is responsible for site performance, trial delivery and Sponsor oversight across assigned countries. The CTLM ensures timely, high‑quality clinical trial execution, with a strong focus on patient recruitment, protocol adherence, regulatory compliance, and data quality.  

Acting as the primary Sponsor representative (“face of Recordati”) at site level, the CTLM builds and maintains strong relationships with Principal Investigators (PIs) and site teams, supports study start‑up and recruitment, and provides in‑field oversight of CRO activities—particularly CRA performance—as part of Recordati’s Sponsor oversight responsibilities.  

The CTLM serves as a key escalation point for site and investigator issues, including unresolved CRO matters, ensuring timely communication to the appropriate Recordati stakeholders. The role provides hands‑on operational and engagement support to sites, CRO CRAs and trial teams to enable successful trial conduct and sustained site performance.  

Working in close collaboration with cross‑functional project teams, the CTLM ensures adherence to project plans, GCP, regulatory requirements, SOPs and company policies, contributing to Recordati’s positioning as a Sponsor of choice.**Key Responsibilities**
**Site Performance \& Recruitment**  

* Acts as Recordati’s Sponsor ambassador at site level, fostering long term, trust based relationships with PIs and site staff.
* Drives patient recruitment by engaging directly with PIs, understanding site specific enrolment strategies, and supporting recruitment acceleration initiatives.
* Works closely with CRAs and site coordinators to ensure alignment on enrolment plans and site priorities.
* Facilitates collaboration across departments within trial sites to optimise patient identification and enrolment.
* Proactively monitors recruitment metrics and identifies and mitigates risks to enrolment timelines.
  

**Sponsor \& CRO Oversight**  

* Provides Sponsor oversight of CRO monitoring activities, with a particular focus on CRA performance and adherence to study processes.
* Conducts Sponsor Oversight Visits (SOVs) and study progress visits to ensure compliant, high quality monitoring (e.g. SDV, issue management).
* May attend PSVs, SIVs, IMVs and COVs, as defined in collaboration with the Lead Clinical Study Manager (CSM).
* Conducts motivational / booster visits when required to re energise site engagement and performance.
  

**Quality, Compliance \& Issue Management**  

* Ensures data quality and data integrity through on site and remote data review, in compliance with ICH GCP and local regulations.
* Proactively identifies operational and site level issues, driving timely resolution and escalating risks to the Lead CSM as appropriate.
* Serves as a site escalation point for investigator concerns, ensuring effective Sponsor follow up and resolution.
* Maintains accountability for long term site partnerships, providing local intelligence and insights to the study team.
  

**Communication \& Cross functional Collaboration**  

* Establishes regular engagement with PIs to discuss study progress, recruitment plans, and scientific or operational questions.
* Reports site status, performance insights, risks and mitigation strategies to the Lead CSM.
* Works across multiple trials within the assigned region, as required, ensuring consistent Sponsor presence and oversight.
**Required Education**

Bachelor’s degree (or international equivalent) in pharmaceutical sciences, chemistry, biology, medicine, healthcare or a related scientific discipline**Required Skills and Experience**
* Minimum 6 years’ experience in Pharma/Biotech, preferably in roles involving clinical site monitoring, site oversight or site engagement.
* Strong understanding of the healthcare and therapeutic landscape, patient pathways and investigative site operations.
* Proven ability to build and manage relationships with PIs, site staff, CROs and internal stakeholders.
* Demonstrated proactive leadership, with the ability to influence without authority and remove operational barriers.
* Highly developed organisational, analytical and problem solving skills, with strong prioritisation capability.
* Experience with clinical trial systems and tools (e.g. CTMS, eTMF, EDC).
* Experience in global clinical trials; rare disease experience is a strong advantage.
* Valid driver’s licence.
**Technical Competencies**
* Clinical Trial \& Sponsor Oversight


Oversight of site and country performance and CRO in field activities as Sponsor representative.  

* GCP \& Regulatory Compliance


Solid knowledge of ICH GCP and applicable local and international regulations.  

* Site \& Investigator Engagement


Ability to establish trusted, productive relationships with PIs and site teams, facilitating scientific and operational discussions.  

* CRO \& CRA Oversight


Oversight of CRA activities, identification of site level risks, and escalation to regional/Lead CSMs.  

* Recruitment \& Performance Optimisation


Identification of recruitment barriers and contribution to enrolment acceleration strategies.  

* Cross functional Clinical Operations Knowledge


Understanding of the full clinical trial lifecycle, from feasibility to close out.  

* Clinical Systems \& Tools Literacy


Working knowledge of CTMS, eTMF, EDC and MS Office tools to support effective oversight.**Required Behaviours and Competencies**
* Stakeholder \& Relationship Management


Builds strong, trust based relationships internally and externally as Recordati’s primary field representative.  

* Proactive Risk \& Issue Management


Anticipates risks, escalates issues early and supports timely resolution.  

* Effective Communication \& Influence


Communicates clearly, consistently and with impact across sites and internal teams.  

* Accountability \& Ownership


Demonstrates strong ownership of site performance, follow up and closure of actions.  

* Collaboration \& Role Clarity


Works in close partnership with Clinical Study Managers, respecting role boundaries.  

* Problem Solving \& Solution Orientation


Applies a pragmatic, solution focused mindset to operational and site challenges.**Required Languages**
* Fluent written and spoken English, Polish and/or Romanian.
* Additional languages are considered an asset.
**Travel required in %**

Approximately 60% domestic and international travel, with the role based in Milan; travel frequency may vary depending on study requirements and operational needs.  

Candidates must hold the right to work in the hiring country
*At Recordati we believe in people! Inspired by our purpose \- unlocking the full potential of life \- we are committed to creating a diverse environment and cultivating a culture of inclusion. We strive to continually lead with our values and beliefs, enabling our employees to bring their whole selves to work and develop their potential.*


*We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We recruit, develop and reward without regard to, amongst others, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, physical or mental ability, race, ethnicity, political or religious belief.*


*If you are looking to join a company where you can try new things, speak openly, and be bold, we invite you to apply today.*

title: CYS - GCAP Joint Venture Headquarters - Delivery Manager - MBSE
description:
**Job Description:**


Leonardo is a global industrial group, among the main global players in Aerospace, Defence and Security that realises multi\-domain technological capabilities in Helicopters, Aircraft, Aerostructures, Electronics, Cyber Security and Space. With over 60,000 employees worldwide, the company has a solid industrial presence in Italy, the UK, Poland and the US. It also operates in 150 countries through subsidiaries, joint ventures and investments. A key player in major international strategic programmes, it is a technological and industrial partner of governments, defence administrations, institutions and companies.

**Within the GCAP Joint Venture, in the GCAP Technical Leadership UO, we are looking for a Delivery Manager – MBSE for GCAP HQ in Reading (UK).**


This role reports to the Product Owner \- Systems Engineering and is accountable for delivering and continuously improving the digital engineering capabilities that support the MBSE pillar. The focus is on enabling the toolchains, digital thread, and lifecycle environments that underpin model\-based systems engineering across the enterprise, ensuring authoritative models replace traditional Office documents or 2D CAD as the primary means of design, communication, and collaboration.


The Product Delivery Manager defines and brings into service the reference architectures and technical baselines for MBSE digital solutions (including Cameo System Modeler, SysML/UML modeling languages, integration with requirements management tool IBM DOORS NG and TeamCenter PLM, configuration and change control, test data management, and evidence capture). The role translates business and engineering needs into a clear capability roadmap and prioritized backlog, and ensures environments deliver end\-to\-end traceability from requirements through models, analyses, and validation, interoperable with enterprise platforms and adjacent pillars.


The role liaises closely with other DE\&D Product Owners and Delivery Managers and with engineering stakeholders to align on standards, interfaces, and adoption, while coordinating boundaries with process and integration owners. Through rigorous backlog management, vendor engagement, and technology evaluations, the Product Delivery Manager ensures the MBSE digital capability delivers the right enabling solutions at the right time in the right way.

**Key Capability Accountabilities and Objectives**

**Product Delivery and Lifecycle Management**

* Own the delivery and continuous improvement of **MBSE digital capabilities**, including toolchains for **SysML/UML modeling**, **requirements management (IBM DOORS NG)**, model repositories, configuration/change control, product line/variant management, and **digital thread connectivity**.
* Ensure **full integration with the DE Digital Thread**, guaranteeing **end‑to‑end traceability** between requirements, models (structure, behavior, interfaces, allocations), analyses, simulation results, verification activities, and test evidence.
* Translate business and engineering needs into **clear product roadmaps**, delivery plans, and **prioritized backlogs** for MBSE capabilities, aligned to program increments and enterprise priorities.
* Establish and promote **best practices** for model‑based engineering: requirement flowdown and allocation, architecture definition, behavior modeling (state/sequence/activity), interface and Interface Control Document management, parametric modeling and constraints, simulation‑based verification, and evidence generation.
* Define and deploy **technical baselines**, **methodologies**, and **work instructions** to enable consistent MBSE ways of working across programs and partners, including integration points with adjacent domains (software/hardware/safety/IVVQ).
* Drive adoption of **models as the authoritative source** across the lifecycle—replacing document‑centric practices (Office files, 2D CAD drawings) for planning, reviews, and cross‑discipline communication.
* Ensure solutions are delivered **on time**, within scope, and aligned with **quality, performance, security, and compliance** targets (including applicable regulatory, safety, and certification expectations).
* Manage upgrades, enhancements, and decommissioning across the digital product lifecycle with **minimal disruption** to dependent engineering teams and governed data migration plans.

**Stakeholder and Business Engagement**

* Capture, challenge, and consolidate **capability requirements** for MBSE to avoid duplication and fragmentation across teams, suppliers, and partner organizations.
* Communicate **product vision**, scope, risks, and progress clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences; maintain transparent roadmaps and release notes.
* Develop and implement **workforce and cultural initiatives** to embed model‑centric behaviors (authoritative models, model reviews, model health metrics), including training materials, onboarding paths, and role‑based enablement.
* Define and design **Domain and Supply Chain access models** and onboarding to MBSE repositories, ensuring appropriate **role‑based access**, data protection, and supplier collaboration patterns.
* Plan and support **business change** for transitioning people and processes to MBSE, including adoption milestones, competency frameworks, coaching, and community of practice facilitation.

**Governance, Standards and Data Quality**

* Ensure adherence to enterprise standards for **MBSE reference architectures**, environment baselines, **configuration \& variant management**, and digital thread enablement.
* Define and enforce **data models**, taxonomies, naming conventions, **metadata standards**, and access controls for SysML/UML models, requirements repositories, simulations, test artefacts, and compliance evidence.
* Establish **model governance** (reviews, sign‑off criteria, health checks, style guides, profiles/stereotypes), **baselining**, and **branching strategies** to maintain digital continuity and auditability.
* Support **regulatory, quality, and audit** requirements related to engineering data, traceability, and evidence pathways (e.g., safety cases, assurance cases, qualification/certification submissions).
* **Delivery Execution and Operational Excellence**
* Coordinate delivery across internal teams and suppliers, ensuring **clear ownership**, accountability, and effective **hand‑offs** within MBSE scope.
* Monitor **service performance**, capacity, availability, **technical debt**, risks, and dependencies across modeling repositories, integration bridges, and verification pipelines.
* Drive incident resolution, root cause analysis, and **continuous service improvement** for MBSE capabilities; implement observability and SLOs for critical services.
* Balance short‑term delivery pressures with **long‑term architectural strategy**, maintainability, scalability, and robust **data stewardship**; guard against tool sprawl and redundant integrations.

* **Vendor and Technology Management**
* Support relationship management with key technology vendors and system integrators for **MBSE platforms**, **IBM DOORS NG**, configuration management, and simulation orchestration tools.
* Support **contract management**, license optimization, and vendor performance reviews to ensure cost‑effective and compliant operation across programs.
* Contribute to **technology evaluations**, proofs of concept, and strategic tool selection to maintain an aligned, interoperable, and **future‑ready MBSE capability**.
* Drive technology innovation in **automation**, model verification, simulation integration/co‑simulation, digital twin alignment, and **evidence generation** to improve speed, quality, and assurance.

**Essential Qualifications**

* Degree in **Systems Engineering**, Engineering (Mechanical/Electrical/Aerospace), Computer Science, or related technical discipline.
* Service Management certification (e.g., **ITIL**) or equivalent experience operating critical engineering services.
* Project/Programme Management certification (e.g., **APM, PRINCE2**, or **SAFe/Agile**) or demonstrable experience managing complex delivery environments.
* **Minimum 10 years** of experience delivering **MBSE capabilities** (SysML/UML modeling, requirements flowdown, architecture \& interface management, behavior/parametric modeling, verification \& validation, simulation integration).
* Proven experience in **Aerospace/Defence** or other **regulated, safety‑critical industries**, with understanding of regulatory and assurance frameworks.
* Strong knowledge of **model‑based engineering** and **digital thread** enablement across requirements, architecture/design, analysis/simulation, manufacturing constraints, test, and compliance evidence.
* Demonstrated ability to manage **multi‑partner and supplier integration** within complex programmes and distributed teams.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence and align diverse teams toward common objectives.
* Strong financial and resource management skills, including planning, budgeting, and cost control for digital engineering capabilities.

**Language Skills:** English C1\.

**Seniority:**


**Primary Location:**


IT \- Napoli \- Pomigliano**Additional Locations:**


IT \- Genova \- Fiumara, IT \- Milano \- Nerviano, IT \- Roma \- Via Laurentina**Contract Type:**


Permanent**Hybrid Working:**

title: Operations Lead - Therapist Education
description:
From day one at Unobravo, we’ve been **on a mission to make mental health support truly accessible to everyone** by normalizing therapy, fostering openness, and breaking down the stigma that still surrounds it.  

Our success is driven by an exceptional team dedicated to providing high\-quality, accessible care, anytime, anywhere. With over 9,000 qualified therapists and more than 7 million therapy sessions delivered, **we’re proud to have made a meaningful difference in countless lives.**


We are looking for an Operations Lead that will drive the operational excellence behind therapist education and success, building and maintaining the programs, workflows, and support structures that enable our therapists to grow continuously and deliver high\-quality care at scale.

 **What You'll Do**

* Identify and assess therapists' education and clinical support needs, translating insights into concrete initiatives that drive meaningful impact on therapist outcomes andexperience.
* Monitor daily operational performance through dedicated dashboards, defining and maintaining thresholds, managing issues as they arise and leading root\-cause analyses to implement lasting preventive improvements.
* Collaborate with the Clinical members and psychologist community to design, develop, and deliver training programs aligned with both clinical relevance and market demand.
* Plan, coordinate, and execute courses end\-to\-end, maintaining an updated activitycalendar and overseeing registration, reporting, and secretariat operations.
* Oversee the full accreditation lifecycle for educational programs, ensuring regulatory compliance and acting as primary liaison with external agencies and vendors.
* Drive the initiation and execution of new projects to enhance the educational offer for psychologists, ensuring timelines and deliverables are met.

### **Must\-have**

* 3–5 years of experience in Operations, Service Operations, or Program Management,


ideally in a fast\-paced, high\-volume scale\-up or a prominent consulting firm.
* Bachelor's degree required (Management, Economics, or related field); Master's preferred.
* Analytical and structured problem\-solver, with hands\-on experience in root\-cause analysis and driving process improvements in high\-variability environments.
* Proven ability to manage complex, cross\-functional projects end\-to\-end, coordinating multiple stakeholders, timelines, and deliverables simultaneously.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with a track record of managing diverse stakeholder relationships, including external partners and clients.
* High ownership mindset, proactive, accountable, and comfortable operating with autonomy in ambiguous, fast\-moving contexts.

### **Power\-Ups**

* Familiarity with accreditation processes, standards, and regulations governing


healthcare or professional education programs is a strong advantage.
* Previous experience in project management roles or operational roles within the education industry.

***Not everyone checks every box, and that’s okay. If this role inspires you and you’re passionate about making an impact in our mission, we’d love to hear from you***

### **What we offer**

##### **Flexibility That Works for You**


You can **work from anywhere** within your country of hire, as long as it’s a safe location with a **suitable workspace**.  

After an**in\-person onboarding week**at our HQ in Milan (travel’s on us), you’re free to choose the place that inspires your best work!

**Desk Here, Desk There**  

Home or hub? You choose! We’ll support you with a **home workstation budget** to perfect your setup and **up to two coworking sessions a month** to connect with your teammates.

 **Scaling with purpose**  

Step into a work environment that's in **constant motion**, and **ever\-evolving**. Be part of a company that's on the rise!

 **Everyone Needs Unobravo**  

Your mental health matters. Get access to **exclusive discounts on psychotherapy sessions** with our professionals: your well\-being comes first.

 **Moments That Matter**  

Enjoy unforgettable**company retreats**,**team\-building**experiences, nationwide **aperitivo parties**and more.. Because we love celebrating milestones together!

 **Free Online Language Training ️**  

We speak your language, and want you to speak ours! Enjoy a **free membership and conversation courses** on our language platform to grow personally and professionally.

**Birthday Off**  

Celebrating the passing of time is also fun at Unobravo! All employees can enjoy a **day off** on the month of their **birthday**.

 **World Mental Health Day, Our Way ‍️**  

On World Mental Health Day, we step back together, giving each of us **an additional day off** to rest, reflect and recharge.

 **Inclusive Parental Leave**  

Enjoy **extra days off** to celebrate the arrival of your little ones. Available for **all parents**, including biological fathers, same\-sex parents, adoptive, and foster parents. Because every family deserves time to connect.

### **To Fit in Our Culture**

  

At Unobravo, what truly connects us isn’t just *what* we do — it’s *how* we do it.  

If you’re curious to discover the values and behaviors that guide us every day, take a look at our **Culture Page** and see what being part of Unobravo really means.

### **Come as you are**

  

At Unobravo, everyone is welcome. We value individuality and believe that diverse perspectives make us stronger.  

We’re proud to be an equal\-opportunity employer and to provide reasonable accommodations to ensure a fair and accessible hiring process for all.  

If you require any support or adjustments during the application or interview process, please let us know, we’ll do our best to make sure you have a positive experience.

### **️ Selection Process**

  

Every application is carefully reviewed by our Talent team. If your profile matches the role, we’ll reach out to schedule an introductory call.  

Our selection journey may vary depending on the position, but it’s always designed to be clear, engaging, and meaningful. You might be invited to complete a case study and will meet your future manager and key team members along the way, so you can get a real sense of what it’s like to work with us, because finding the right fit goes both ways.  

And don’t worry! You’ll always have a dedicated point of contact from our Talent team to guide you through each step and make sure you have the best possible experience.

title: Project Leader - R&D Engineer
description:
**Project Leader \- R\&D Engineer**

  

At Fresenius Medical Care, we are the global leader in kidney care, committed to improving the lives of patients with chronic kidney disease. Our mission is to deliver high\-quality, innovative products, services, and care across the entire healthcare journey. Guided by our vision—“Creating a future worth living. For patients. Worldwide. Every day.”—we work with purpose and compassion, supported by a global team of over 125,000 employees.


Within our Care Enablement segment, we develop and provide life\-sustaining medical products, digital health solutions, and therapies that empower clinical teams and improve patient outcomes. Our FME Reignite strategy drives transformation through innovation, efficiency, and sustainable growth.


Our values guide how we work:


We Care for our patients, each other, and our communities  

We Connect across teams and borders to deliver excellence together  

We Commit to doing things the right way— growing with purpose and leading kidney care with integrity and innovation

 **Information about department**


In\-Center as one of three therapy verticals focuses on delivering high\-quality, clinic\-based hemodialysis to patients worldwide. Its mission is to support healthcare providers in offering safe, consistent, and patient\-centered care in dialysis clinics.


The team collaborates across Product Design, Clinical Protocols, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, Quality, Regulatory, and Commercial functions to equip clinics with effective, innovative solutions that meet high standards and improve patient outcomes. Join our passionate team and help shape healthcare worldwide!

**Summary about role**


As Project Leader \- R\&D Engineer, you will lead projects for developing and optimizing disposable medical devices, ensuring compliance with product specifications and patient safety. You will manage assigned tasks to meet agreed targets while adhering to company procedures. Quality and safety standards must be respected throughout all activities.

**Your Responsibilities**


Lead end\-to\-end projects from concept to production readiness, ensuring compliance with quality and safety standards.  

Manage design input, verification, validation, and transfer activities using advanced tools (e.g., Windchill).  

Optimize and sustain existing products globally within the Value Stream.  

Define and update product requirements and technical specifications.  

Coordinate cross\-functional teams (R\&D, Manufacturing, Regulatory, QA, Marketing) to deliver on time and within scope.  

Drive risk assessment, mitigation strategies, and structured project plans to meet milestones.  

Act as Product Interface Manager, ensuring seamless documentation and implementation.

 **Your Profile**


Master’s degree or similar education level in biomedical engineering/mechanical engineering discipline.  

Project management training or qualifications is desirable.  

Minimum 3 years’ experience in project lead/management.  

Experienced in the use of Project management tools and techniques to develop schedules, allocate resources, and track progress.  

Basic knowledge about plastic materials and relative production\- and assembly technologies.  

English (level B2\).  

Leadership skills and experience leading high\-performing technical teams.  

Takes accountability for and delivers consistent decisions, actions, and results.  

Ability to take decisions in difficult situations.

 **Our Offer for you**


There is a lot you can discover at Fresenius Medical Care, regardless in which field you are an expert and how much experience you have \- all dedicated to your professional journey.


Whether in front of or behind the scenes \- you are helping to make ever better medicine available to more and more people around the world  

Individual opportunities for self\-determined career planning and professional development  

A corporate culture in which there is enough room for innovative thinking \- to find the best solution together, not the quickest one  

A large number of committed people with a wide range of skills, talents and experience

title: Tender Manager (m/f/x)
description:
**Entity:** CSL Behring


**Job Category:** Commercial


**Job Family:** Tender Management


**Job Type:** Full Time


**Location****:** Milan, Lombardia, Italy


**Employer Reference:** R\-275058


**Job Description**
-------------------


**Tender Manager (m/f/x)**
==========================

**Location:** Milan, Italy

**Contract Type:** Permanent contract, Fulltime  

**Department:** Commercial  

**Reporting to:** Head of Market Access \& Value

**About the Role**


We are looking for a **Tender Manager** to join our Commercial Department in Italy.  

This role is responsible for the end\-to\-end management of public tenders and commercial offers, ensuring accurate product positioning, competitive pricing, and full compliance with local and global requirements.


The Tender Manager plays a key role in monitoring tender opportunities, supporting pricing and access strategies, coordinating internal and external stakeholders, and providing analytical insights to support business decisions.

**Your Responsibilities**

* Manage the end\-to\-end planning, coordination, and submission of public tenders and commercial offers, ensuring accuracy, competitiveness, and compliance.
* Monitor and analyze tender opportunities, providing strategic input on participation and commercial conditions.
* Coordinate internal and external stakeholders, including tender service agencies, local functions, and global tender and pricing teams.
* Manage tender documentation and administrative requirements in line with company procedures and regulatory standards.
* Update and manage SAP contracts, pricing data, and official price lists following tender outcomes and price changes.
* Monitor tender awards, extensions, and contract expiration dates, ensuring timely reporting and proactive management.
* Support pricing and market access strategy through tender performance analysis and reporting.
* Ensure compliance with quality, governance, and pharmacovigilance requirements, including ISO indicators, Organismo di Vigilanza documentation, and mandatory training.
**Your Profile**

* University degree in Economics, Business Administration, or another relevant discipline.
* Minimum **5 years of experience** in public tenders and/or pricing and reimbursement.
* Solid experience within the **Healthcare System** environment.
* Previous experience in the **pharmaceutical or biotechnology industry**.
* Strong knowledge of public procurement and tender processes.
* Excellent analytical, organizational, and reporting skills.
* High attention to detail and strong compliance mindset.
* Ability to work effectively in a cross‑functional and matrix organization.
* Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
* Experience with SAP and tender management tools.

**Why Join Us**


You will be part of a collaborative and highly specialized team, working at the intersection of Market Access, Pricing, and Commercial strategy, with significant exposure to both local and global stakeholders. This role offers the opportunity to have a direct impact on business performance within a dynamic and regulated healthcare environment.

**What We Offer**


We offer an open\-end contract (Tempo Indeterminato) regulated by the CCNL Chimico Farmaceutico integrated by an outstanding benefit pack for the role as well as an annual merit reward process and individual development plan.


**About CSL Behring**
---------------------


CSL Behring is a global biotherapeutics leader driven by our promise to save lives. Focused on serving patients' needs by using the latest technologies, we discover, develop and deliver innovative therapies for people living with conditions in the immunology, hematology, cardiovascular and metabolic, respiratory, and transplant therapeutic areas. We use three strategic scientific platforms of plasma fractionation, recombinant protein technology, and cell and gene therapy to support continued innovation and continually refine ways in which products can address unmet medical needs and help patients lead full lives.


CSL Behring operates one of the world's largest plasma collection networks, CSL Plasma. Our parent company, CSL, headquartered in Melbourne, Australia, employs 32,000 people, and delivers its lifesaving therapies to people in more than 100 countries.


To learn more about CSL, CSL Behring, CSL Seqirus and CSL Vifor visit https://www.csl.com/ and CSL Plasma at https://www.cslplasma.com/.



**Our Benefits**
----------------


For more information on CSL benefits visit How CSL Supports Your Well\-being \| CSL.



**You Belong at CSL**
---------------------


At CSL, Inclusion and Belonging is at the core of our mission and who we are. It fuels our innovation day in and day out. By celebrating our differences and creating a culture of curiosity and empathy, we are able to better understand and connect with our patients and donors, foster strong relationships with our stakeholders, and sustain a diverse workforce that will move our company and industry into the future.


To learn more about inclusion and belonging visit https://www.csl.com/careers/inclusion\-and\-belonging



**Equal Opportunity Employer**
------------------------------


CSL is an Equal Opportunity Employer. If you are an individual with a disability and need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, please visit https://www.csl.com/accessibility\-statement.

title: Treasury Specialist
description:
The new E.ON is one of the strongest operators of European energy networks and energy\-related infrastructure, as well as a provider of advanced customer solutions for more than 50 million customers. With a total of over 75,000 employees, we are represented in 15 countries. This makes us ideally positioned to help drive Europe's energy transition. By focusing on two sustainable growth areas fully commits itself to the fundamental transformation of the energy world. As one of Europe’s largest energy service providers we will actively drive the energy transition in Italy and Europe. E.ON is a pioneer in developing new and innovative energy solutions and attract the brightest minds in the energy sector.


**Our Team**


Within our treasury and finance operations, your work directly ensures financial reliability, liquidity transparency, and process excellence. The team operates at the crossroads of banking, accounting, and internal stakeholders, where taking full ownership of tasks and delivering tangible results is key to shared success. Close collaboration across departments enables efficient decision\-making and supports business objectives with trusted data. Knowledge sharing and mutual support drive our ability to continuously improve treasury processes together.

**Your Field of Play**

* **Cash collection, accounting reconciliation, and KPI monitoring**  

Your expertise ensures incoming payments are managed accurately, reconciliations are carried out reliably, and monthly KPIs are monitored with a strong analytical focus to support decision‑making and data‑driven problem solving
* **Execution of treasury operations and payment processes**  

Recurring treasury activities, bank accounting, and payment executions are handled by you with precision, ensuring data quality and on‑time delivery that consistently meets operational standards and stakeholder expectations
* **Managing banking documentation within KYC and AML frameworks**  

In your hands lies the structured collection, maintenance, and updating of banking and compliance documentation, supporting regulatory readiness and transparent cooperation with financial partners
* **Driving process efficiency through treasury initiatives and projects**  

Continuous improvement is actively shaped by you through involvement in special treasury projects, implementation of new products, and optimisation initiatives, recognising change as an opportunity to enhance effectiveness
* **Cross‑functional collaboration to achieve shared goals**  

By working closely with internal departments, you contribute to end‑to‑end process thinking, actively sharing insights to deliver exceptional internal customer outcomes and support company‑wide objectives

**Are You Our Playmaker?**

* **Education in finance, economics, or a comparable field**  

A diploma or bachelor’s degree provides the foundational knowledge required to understand treasury processes and financial operations in depth
* **Solid experience in treasury and banking processes**  

Your understanding of cash management, payments, reconciliations, and recurring treasury activities enables you to operate confidently and independently
* **Strong analytical and organisational capabilities**  

Complex data is analysed by you with structure and clarity, allowing potential issues to be identified early and solved effectively through sound problem‑solving skills
* **Hands‑on proficiency with MS Office, especially Excel**  

Daily work benefits from your confident use of Excel for analysis and reporting, paired with openness to learning new tools and digital solutions
* **Experience with treasury systems and banking platforms**  

Familiarity with SAP, Inbiz, or comparable remote banking systems supports efficient execution, while knowledge of Tagetik, FIS, Neta, or Salesforce CRM is considered an advantage
* **Collaborate effectively to achieve shared goals and drive growth**  

Shared success is created through your ability to work closely with colleagues across functions, actively contributing knowledge and supporting collective outcomes
* **Take full ownership of tasks and deliver tangible results**  

Responsibility for quality, timelines, and outcomes is embraced by you, even in challenging situations, ensuring consistent and reliable performance
* **Clear communication skills with strong English proficiency**  

Financial topics are communicated by you in a structured and audience‑appropriate way, enabling effective collaboration in an international environment


**Do you have questions?**


If you are convinced that this is the next step in your career, register and apply online (Job Req ID: 244179 ).  

Shaping the future of energy together!

**What you need to know:**


Contract type: Permanent  

Working time: Full time  

Company: E.ON Italia S.p.A.  

Function area: Finance/Accounting/Tax


This job posting is open to all genders, in compliance with Legislative Decree No. 198 of April 11, 2006, “Code of Equal Opportunities between Men and Women,” and to individuals of all ages and nationalities, in compliance with EU Directive 2000/78/EC on equal treatment in employment

title: Sr Product Specialist, Market Data Procurement & Vendor Ops
description:
Since our founding in 1996, we have been at the vanguard of financial technology, providing groundbreaking expertise, quantitative analytics and software that redefine pricing and risk management in the financial markets. With the strategic acquisitions of FINCAD, PolyPaths and Kynex, Numerix has further strengthened its leadership position empowering financial institutions worldwide, to transform risk into opportunities with confidence.



Numerix's data vendor landscape has grown to a scale where **vendor operations require dedicated ownership**. The **Senior Product Specialist, Market Data Procurement \& Vendor Operations** role exists to own that problem space end to end—covering vendor planning, licensing, audits, renewals, compliance, and rationalization.



The data entering the platform is sourced through provider partnerships that have taken years to build, many offering coverage not publicly available. These relationships are a core competitive advantage for Numerix Data Management (NxDM), and this role exists to own, maintain, and strengthen them. This role sits within the Product Management team and focuses on ensuring the quality, coverage, and cost\-efficiency of the data that powers every pricing model, risk calculation, and analytics workflow in the Numerix product suite that clients depend on for regulated decision\-making.


**Success in this role** means NxDM's data provider ecosystem is proactively maintained, grows as a strategic asset, coverage gaps are closed at source, provider contracts and licensing are managed with full visibility, and the data entering the platform is evaluated for readiness to support quantitative finance and AI workflows.

  

**What You'll** **Do****:**


**Vendor Operations** (Primary Focus)


* Own end‑to‑end execution of data vendor operations across all Numerix vendors, including Numerix, FINCAD, PolyPaths, Kynex, and future acquisitions.
* Maintain full visibility of vendor contracts, including renewals, termination windows, pricing, and redistribution rights.
* Prepare commercial groundwork for vendor negotiations (usage data, cost analysis, contract review, renewal briefs); negotiations and signature led by senior leadership.
* Run vendor audits end‑to‑end, coordinating entitlements, Legal, and vendor engagement.
* Manage licensing compliance, usage monitoring, audit readiness, vendor onboarding, and invoice discrepancies.
* Maintain a single, consolidated view of total data vendor spend and justification.
* Identify vendor overlap and rationalization opportunities, including duplicate coverage and consolidation options.


**NxDM** **Data Procurement Domain** **Ownership**


* Maintain a living view of NxDM data coverage (vendors, asset classes, instruments, feeds, depth)
* Acts as the fastest reference for: "Do we have this data, and if not, how do we get it?"
* Drive resolution of recurring data quality or coverage issues that stall in vendor support by engaging provider product teams directly and pushing for structural fixes at the source.
* Evaluate vendor capabilities for new clients, prospects, or asset class requirements. Work with NxDM's quantitative and domain specialists to validate fit, including readiness for quantitative finance and AI\-driven workflows.
* Own the internal data procurement roadmap (coverage gaps, feed onboarding, vendor changes, new evaluations).
* Translate vendor platform changes into NxDM impact assessments and sprint\-ready inputs.


**Pre****Sales** **\& Internal Enablement**


* Support PreSales and Professional Services with licensing, redistribution, coverage, and cost questions.
* Handle straightforward cross\-product data requests where processing is minimal.
* Provide a regular vendor summary for Product and Finance covering spend, renewals, compliance, audits, and rationalization.


**Cross****‑****Product Collaboration**



Partner with other Product Managers on:


* Vendor licensing and contract questions related to their data sources
* Vendor rationalization across products
* New vendor onboarding where the PM defines the need and this role executes

  


**What We're Looking For:**


* 5 \- 8 years of experience in capital markets technology, market data, or financial services, with direct exposure to how market data flows into pricing, risk, or trading workflows.
* Bachelor's degree in a quantitative or finance\-related discipline (Finance, Economics, Financial Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or related field). A postgraduate degree is valued but not required.
* Experience working with market data providers such as Bloomberg, Refinitiv/LSEG, ICE, S\&P Global, FactSet, or similar, in a product, data management, partnerships, or technical capacity. Experience on the provider side is a strong advantage.
* Capital markets fluency: you understand what an interest rate swap curve is, why a volatility surface matters, and what data completeness means in a regulatory context. You do not need to be a quant, but you need enough domain knowledge to hold credible conversations with provider product teams and internal quantitative specialists.
* Familiarity with data provider contract lifecycle management, licensing models, redistribution and revenue\-sharing agreements, and audit processes in a financial service or fintech context.
* Product thinking: able to own a domain roadmap, write clear requirements, prioritise competing demands, and work with engineering teams on delivery.
* Strong analytical and communication skills, with the ability to synthesise complex data quality, coverage, or licensing issues into clear, actionable assessments for both technical and non\-technical audiences.
* Comfortable working across a wide range of stakeholders: provider product managers, account managers, salespeople, as well as internal engineering, pre\-sales, finance, legal, and product teams.
* Self\-motivated, execution\-oriented, and comfortable in environments where processes are still being built.
* Familiarity with data governance frameworks or regulatory data requirements (e.g., BCBS 239\) is a plus.
* Awareness of how AI and machine learning are changing market data products and provider offerings is a plus.

  



**Where You'll Work:**  

  

This is a hybrid role, open to those who live in the areas of London, UK, New York, USA (midtown Manhattan), Toronto (GTA) or Milan, Italy.

  

**An important note on salary:**



The annual pay range for this position is based on the preferred primary location of the role which is listed above. If you are applying to this role at a location that is not the preferred primary location, please keep in mind the salary range will vary and may fall outside of what is listed. Base pay offered may vary depending on job\-related knowledge, skills, and experience.

  

***Also note that unsolicited contact from third\-party recruiters or agencies will not be considered at this time. We respectfully request no outreach from agencies.***

title: EH&S Specialist M-F
description:
Published 04\.23\.2026
 Company : Safran Electronics \& Defense Job field : Health \& Safety, Environment Location : Brugherio , Italy Contract type : Permanent Contract duration : Full\-time Required degree : Master Degree Required experience : More than 3 years
\# 2026\-177976

Published 04\.23\.2026
**Job Description**
-------------------


We are looking for an EH\&S Specialist to join our Brugherio site operating in the aerospace sector, a highly regulated and technologically advanced environment where safety, precision, and compliance are critical to operational excellence.  

  

In this role, you will play a key part in ensuring that our Environment, Health \& Safety standards fully support the reliability, quality, and innovation required by the aerospace industry. You will act as a trusted partner to the organization, promoting a strong safety culture and contributing to sustainable and compliant operations across the site.  

  

This position offers the opportunity to work in a complex manufacturing environment, interacting with cross\-functional teams and external stakeholders, and directly impacting employee wellbeing, environmental protection, and regulatory compliance. You will contribute not only to maintaining high safety standards but also to continuously improving processes in line with both local regulations and international aerospace requirements.  

  

If you are motivated by working in a challenging, high\-performance industrial context where safety is a core value and operational discipline makes the difference, this role offers strong exposure, autonomy, and professional growth.


**Job Requirements**
--------------------


Key Responsibilities  

  

* Ensure site compliance with applicable EH\&S regulations and corporate requirements.
* Support risk assessments and the implementation of preventive and corrective actions.
* Design, implement, and continuously improve EH\&S programs, procedures, and initiatives.
* Work closely with the EHS teams across the sites to drive alignment and promote the implementation of shared projects and initiatives.
* Monitor site performance against regulatory requirements and corporate KPIs.
* Manage environmental and health \& safety compliance activities, including managing and maintaining ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 management systems, ensuring ongoing compliance with certification requirements. This includes supporting internal and external audits, maintaining documented information, monitoring corrective and preventive actions, tracking performance indicators, and driving continuous improvement initiatives aligned with corporate standards.
* Prepare and submit environmental regulatory reports and maintain accurate documentation.
* Develop clear and effective reports and presentations tailored to different levels of the organization, from operators to senior management.
* Develop, deliver, and track EH\&S training programs and toolbox talks.
* Partner with business functions to support safe work practices and regulatory compliance.
* Coordinate with cross\-functional teams to address EH\&S topics related to facilities, contractors, and employee activities.
* Support contractor management processes to ensure safety and regulatory compliance.
* Contribute to the development and communication of EH\&S policies and procedures across the site.


  

Professional Requirements  

  

* Promote a strong safety culture across all levels of the organization.
* Support the implementation of corporate EH\&S programs and campaigns.
* Proven experience managing small projects or EH\&S processes.
* Strong knowledge of environmental and health \& safety regulations.
* Strong ability to interact and collaborate with different company functions, including management, supervisors, operators, and other stakeholders.
* Ability to design and deliver effective EH\&S training sessions.
* Detail\-oriented, with strong organizational skills in managing training records, deadlines, and compliance documentation.

  

  

Soft Skills  

* Proactive and solution\-oriented mindset.
* Strong communication and influencing skills.
* Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities.
* Good analytical and problem\-solving skills.


  

Education  

  

* Degree in Engineering (Environmental Engineering preferred).
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools.
* At least 2 years of experience in similar roles or activities.

  

  

Languages  

* Fluent in English.
* Basic proficiency in French is considered an asset.

**Company Information**
-----------------------


Safran is an international high\-technology group, operating in the aviation (propulsion, equipment and interiors), defense and space markets. Its core purpose is to contribute to a safer, more sustainable world, where air transport is more environmentally friendly, comfortable and accessible. Safran has a global presence, with 100,000 employees and sales of 27\.3 billion euros in 2024, and holds, alone or in partnership, world or regional leadership positions in its core markets.  

Safran is in the 2nd place in the aerospace and defense industry in TIME magazine's "World's best companies 2024" ranking.
  

  

Safran Electronics \& Defense offers its customers onboard intelligence solutions allowing them to understand the environment, reduce mental load and guarantee a trajectory, even in critical situations, in all environments: on land, at sea, in the sky or space. The company harnesses the expertise of its 13,000 employees towards these three functions: observe, decide and guide, for the civil and military markets.

title: Public Procurement & Tender Specialist
description:
***About Fondazione Chips\-IT***

*The Foundation “Italian Center for the Design of Semiconductor Integrated Circuits,” also known as the Chips\-IT Foundation, is a nonprofit research and technology organization under the supervision of the Ministries of Industry.*

*The Foundation is Italy's first RTO (Research and Technology Organization) vertically focused on semiconductor research and stands as a center of excellence in frontier research on semiconductor design, as well as a pivotal center of the Italian semiconductor ecosystem and expertise.*

 *Missions of the Foundation:*

* Promote the design and development of integrated circuits
* Strengthen the system of professional training in the field of microelectronics
* Ensure the establishment of a network of universities, research centers and enterprises that fosters innovation and technology transfer in the field

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

Fondazione Chips\-IT is seeking a **Public Procurement \& Tender Specialist** to join the Procurement and Contract Management area.

  

The selected candidate will support the analysis, preparation, and management of public procurement procedures, contracts, and administrative acts in compliance with **Italian Public Procurement Law (D.Lgs. 36/2023 – Codice dei Contratti Pubblici).**


The role involves close collaboration with internal departments and the RUP (Responsabile Unico del Progetto), with the expectation that the candidate will **assume the RUP role in the short term**. This position offers the opportunity to grow professionally in a structured and international research environment, contributing to the correct management of tender and contract activities across the Foundation.

 **Candidates with consolidated experience in public procurement, able to operate autonomously, are also encouraged to apply.**

  

This position is based in**Pavia**, with interaction with all Fondazione Chips\-IT operational units.

  

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

* Public Procurement Support:Support public procurement procedures for works, services, and supplies in compliance with D.Lgs. 36/2023
* Tender Documentation:Assist in the preparation of tender documentation, including determine a contrarre, disciplinari di gara, contracts, and administrative acts
* E\-Procurement Platforms:Operate e\-procurement platforms (MEPA, Traspare, SINTEL or similar) for tender publication and document management
* RUP Support:Provide operational support to the RUP throughout procurement phases, from planning to execution
* Tender Committees:Support tender committees and assist in the preparation of official minutes
* Compliance \& Monitoring:Monitor deadlines and documentation to ensure regulatory compliance and proper contract management

**Requisiti:**
--------------

* Experience:Proven track record (minimum 10–12 months) in public procurement, gained either within a Contracting Authority (Stazione Appaltante) or a Private Operator

-----------------------

description:
Milano, Italy \| Full time \| Hybrid \| R1521039
  
**Senior AI Engineer****Data Science \& Advanced Analytics**
Working with an enormous breadth of data, you will be able to design and develop ML and data science applications. As a Senior Engineer you will collaborate with data scientists, business owners, subject matter experts, and go to market teams to build truly innovative solutions to support innovative AIML driven solutions.
Involved in every stage of the product development lifecycle, you will work with data scientists to turn working prototypes into well\-abstracted reusable services, build software to expose data science models to business users, and support a culture of continuous improvement. As a senior software engineer, you will collaborate with Development team for architecture and coding standards.**Your typical activities will include:*** Consistently deliver robust, high\-quality, reliable engineering outcomes in cross functional autonomous scrum teams
* Develop automated CI/CD pipelines that deploy to both on\-premises clusters and to the cloud
* Research current and emerging industry tools and techniques, and share these findings with colleagues
* Improve product quality
* Coordinate with data scientists and facilitating their development with the aim of helping them write production grade code
* Establish and enforce standards
* Facilitate cross\-team knowledge sharing
* Collaborate with engineers and leaders from other organizations inside IQVIA
* Lead documentation efforts
* Perform code reviews for work submitted by data scientists
* Apply SDLC processes consistently every day
* Mentor data scientists and other engineers

**Qualification:*** Substantial relevant collaborative software engineering experience
* Previous full time engineering roles in at least two companies
* Solid understanding of computer science fundamentals
* Expert familiarity with backend development in Python
* Experience designing applications using a microservices architecture
* Experience with LangChain, LangGraph, or similar LLM orchestration frameworks.
* Experience deploying models in cloud environments (AWS, GCP, or Azure).
* Knowledge of Kubernetes or another container orchestration platform
* Experience with relational databases
* Experience working with large volumes of complex data with workflow management tools like Airflow
* Strong understanding of software engineering principles, version control, and testing.
* Experience deploying code into production through CI/CD tools
* Experience building RESTful APIs
* Good verbal and written communication skills
* Knowledge of Agile methodology and experience working in a collaborative Agile environment

**What We Offer*** The opportunity to work on transformative AI solutions in healthcare and pharma.
* A collaborative, research\-driven environment with real\-world impact.
* Career growth through innovation, mentorship, and continuous learning.
* Competitive compensation and benefits

**The Team**
Data Science \& Advanced Analytics – with departments in Frankfurt, Philadelphia, Milan, Madrid, Athens, Warsaw and Beijing as well as a network of more than 300 experts worldwide \- is the global competence center for statistics and data science at IQVIA. Data Science \& Advanced Analytics is responsible for developments of the statistical procedures and methods used for quality assurance processes, data imputations, data projections, forecasting and many other for healthcare panel data including pharmacy sales, ecommerce data, hospital consumption, physician level prescriptions and longitudinal anonymized patient level data.
Joining the Data Science \& Advanced Analytics team provides the opportunity to work with large and complex data and methodologies in a fast\-paced, ever\-changing environment to support international customers. As a member of our team you can expect exciting international projects with interesting development perspectives.**Why Join?**
Those who join us become part of a recognized global leader still willing to challenge the status quo to improve patient care. You will have access to the most cutting\-edge technology, the largest data sets, the best analytics tools and, in our opinion, some of the finest minds in the Healthcare industry.
You can drive your career at IQVIA and choose the path that best defines your development and success. With exposure across diverse geographies, capabilities, and vast therapeutic and information and technology areas, you can seek opportunities to change and grow without boundaries.
We know that meaningful results require not only the right approach but also the right people. Regardless of your role, we invite you to reimagine healthcare with us. You will have the opportunity to play an important part in helping our clients drive healthcare forward and ultimately improve human health outcomes.**It's an exciting time to join and reimagine what's possible in healthcare.***IQVIA is a strong advocate of diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We believe that a work environment that embraces diversity will give us a competitive advantage in the global marketplace and enhance our success. We believe that an inclusive and respectful workplace culture fosters a sense of belonging among our employees, builds a stronger team, and allows individual employees the opportunity to maximize their personal potential*
IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com
IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.

title: Site Manager
description:
At Unispace, we are committed to transforming workplaces to meet the diverse needs of today's dynamic global brands. Our agile, end\-to\-end approach is delivered seamlessly across borders, fueled by strong client partnerships and supported by insightful workplace intelligence and innovative technology.



We believe that everyone deserves to work in an environment that inspires productivity, fosters peak performance, and promotes overall well\-being. Across our 48 global studios, we recognize that positive work environments cultivate open, collaborative, and supportive cultures, enriched by the contributions of independent and motivated individuals from all backgrounds.



While our core business revolves around strategizing, designing, and building workplaces for some of the world's leading brands, we understand that our people are our greatest asset. We prioritize their growth and well\-being through a comprehensive Employee Value Proposition program that includes rewards and recognition, career development opportunities, and a strong focus on mental and physical well\-being.



To further support our team, we embrace flexible working arrangements, including hybrid working options that empower our employees to balance in\-office collaboration with remote work flexibility. This approach not only enhances work\-life balance but also allows us to harness the full potential of our diverse and talented workforce.

**Why you're here**



As site manager you will ensure the successful delivery of high\-quality construction projects from start to finish. As a vital member of our team, you will manage on\-site operations, ensuring that subcontractors, stakeholders, and the Unispace team are aligned and that projects are delivered on time, safely, and to the highest standard. Your leadership will help bring our innovative designs to life while maintaining our commitment to safety, quality, and client satisfaction. By championing Unispace's values and methodologies, you will play a crucial role in creating exceptional spaces that exceed client expectations.


**What you're responsible for**


* Gain a thorough understanding of subcontractor contracts, project scope, drawings, and specifications.
* Execute delivery strategies to ensure project success.
* Manage relationships and communication with on\-site stakeholders, including clients, landlords, tenants, and agents.
* Monitor and manage the performance of subcontractors to ensure high\-quality outcomes.
* Provide clear communication with the Unispace team regarding on\-site activities and progress.
* Collaborate across disciplines, regions, and studios to effectively implement Unispace's 80/20 methodology.
* Uphold Unispace's culture, values, and behaviors in all aspects of project management.
* Ensure strict adherence to health and safety standards on\-site.
* Maintain site cleanliness and manage setup and establishment of job sites.
* Oversee program management, ensuring projects stay on schedule and meet all deadlines.
* Complete punch lists and ensure quality control and quality assurance at every stage.
* Maintain accurate on\-site documentation and oversee document control processes.
* Implement site risk management plans and escalate risks or issues to the Senior Project Manager or Project Manager when necessary, following ethical practices.
* Manage the project close\-out process, ensuring smooth completion and handover.
* Support and contribute to the implementation of Unispace's Corporate Social Responsibility program.


**Your experience and skills**


* Relevant experience in construction project management, with a focus on commercial interior office fit\-outs.
* Strong understanding of health and safety standards, with relevant qualifications.
* Knowledge of construction sequencing and building codes.
* Ability to read and understand drawings and specifications.
* Excellent time management skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks effectively.
* Strong communication skills with the ability to communicate with impact across teams and stakeholders.
* Problem\-solving skills and the ability to address challenges proactively.
* Ability to influence others and develop effective teams.
* High attention to detail and commitment to quality.
* Proficiency in project management software systems and Microsoft Office Suite.
* Proven track record of delivering projects successfully and managing subcontractors.


The statements in this job description are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by the person(s) assigned to this job. This job description is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities and duties required.


*We encourage applicants of all backgrounds to apply, and we are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities, allowing them to perform essential job functions as determined on a case\-by\-case basis.*


**Inclusion and Diversity Commitment:**



At Unispace, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We proudly embrace equal opportunity hiring and will evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, religion, color, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, national origin, ancestry, marital status, medical condition (as defined by state law), physical or mental disability, military service, veteran status, pregnancy, childbirth, and related medical conditions, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.



We are proud that our leadership reflects this commitment, with **persons of color, women, neurodiverse and LGBTQ\+ individuals** playing pivotal roles in shaping the future of our organization. Their unique perspectives and experiences drive innovation and help us create a truly inclusive culture. We are dedicated to ensuring that every voice is heard and that our workplace is a space where diversity is not only celebrated but is integral to our success.

title: Senior Director, Professional Services
description:
locations
Vimercate, Italy
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Yesterday
job requisition id
JR110239
Genesys empowers organizations of all sizes to improve loyalty and business outcomes by creating the best experiences for their customers and employees. Through Genesys Cloud, the AI\-powered Experience Orchestration platform, organizations can accelerate growth by delivering empathetic, personalized experiences at scale to drive customer loyalty, workforce engagement, efficiency and operational improvements.


We employ more than 6,000 people across the globe who embrace empathy and cultivate collaboration to succeed. And, while we offer great benefits and perks like larger tech companies, our employees have the independence to make a larger impact on the company and take ownership of their work. Join the team and create the future of customer experience together.


We are seeking a highly experienced leader, for the role of **Senior Director of Professional Services**, to lead **Sales and Delivery** of our **Professional Services business**, as part of the Customer Success organization, based in Milan.


This person is responsible for Professional Services’ business development and delivery, leading their team to **identify, sell and deliver strategic services opportunities** for the organization, generate revenue, and achieve Professional Services’ sales objectives to drive value for their customers. Working with the account teams, you and your team will implement Professional Services business development strategy, identify new business opportunities, create and maintain key customer and partner relationships, and negotiate and close business deals. As part of the Customer Success organization, you will play a leadership role in **driving adoption and growth** at key customers within the region.


You will build a culture of **proactive, programmatic, customer\-centric execution**, overseeing the day\-to\-day delivery of all customer projects in your region and supporting your team to track project status and profitability, manage project resources (people, budget, scope, time), manage client expectations and leverage best practice sales and delivery techniques to deliver your business goals.


You will be responsible for **hiring, developing and retaining talent**, as well as performance management, and will lead a team of Project Managers and Services Engagement Directors (services’ sales reps), developing a **high\-performance culture** within the team.


You will directly engage with the Sales teams and other Genesys functions, at all levels, locally and in other geographies, to achieve your objectives and drive customer success


You will engage with **senior stakeholders and executives** to align business priorities, drive innovation, and ensure measurable value realization.


You will serve as the **senior Genesys leader** embedded in specific accounts, responsible for partnership success and **transformation outcomes**.


You will turn your customers into **champions and advocates** through strong relationships and measurable success.


This role is results\-driven and would suit someone with a **strong technology consulting or customer success leadership background**, with demonstrable experience of leading high\-performing project teams, influencing others and delivering strong results.


Experience in **SaaS organizations**, working with **Cloud technologies, AI solutions** and **agile delivery methodologies**, and delivering **Enterprise Digital Transformation** are desirable.


Italian, Spanish \& English languages are highly desirable.

 **Key Responsibilities:**

  

Client \& Engagement Leadership

* Shape and oversee a portfolio of large, complex engagements or transformation programs.
* Own senior\-level client relationships and act as a trusted advisor to executive stakeholders.
* Deliver and oversee a portfolio of programs, including large \& complex enterprise engagements.
* Foster delivery excellence, maintaining quality, timelines, budget, and client satisfaction, in line with industry project management standards (PMI, PRINCE2, etc)
* Schedule resources, set goals and priorities, change management, reporting project status, tracking and resolving issues and customer acceptance.
* Manage escalation issues and guide teams toward proactive risk mitigation.

  

Business Development \& Growth

* Lead the strategic and tactical planning for Professional Services in your region, in alignment with Professional Services, Customer Success and Genesys goals and strategy.
* Formulate, define and execute sales strategy, with Sales and Customer Success leadership, understanding customer business\- and technical objectives and delivering solutions that drive those objectives
* Articulate, present and negotiate customer proposals, including pricing negotiations
* Create and execute Transformation Roadmaps for customers that deliver continuous incremental value at each step of the journey and support renewals and expansion within the account
* Demonstrate thought leadership and continuously innovate to grow your business and achieve your goals

  

People Leadership \& Talent Development

* Establish and manage a team of Services Engagement Directors (services sales reps) and Project Managers within a fast\-paced, results focused, high\-performance environment, ensuring the team receives continued training and development.
* Foster a culture of continuous learning and performance excellence.
* Conduct performance evaluations and guide career progression for your team.
* Collaborate closely with other functions across Genesys, including Go\-To\-Customer Account Teams, Product, Operations, Marketing, Product Support, and Education, to achieve your Professional Services and wider Customer Success goals.

  

Operational \& Financial Management

* Drive and achieve sales objectives through bookings and orders.
* Achieving profitability goals by providing effective, high\-quality delivery services.
* Building \& negotiating financial proposals.
* Create \& maintain accurate business forecasts, leveraging the appropriate systems.

  

Stakeholder \& Ecosystem Management

* Establish and maintain quality relationships with customers, strategic partners and/or third\-party providers, across business and technology domains.
* Establish and maintain a strong internal network at all levels within Genesys to enable you to achieve your goals.

 **Minimum Requirements:**

 **Essential**

* 10\+ years relevant experience in the IT consulting industry, ideally in the Customer Experience/Contact Centre, AI, Telecommunications and/or CRM markets. \- 8\+ years of supervisory, management, or leadership experience.
* Strong technology consulting background, demonstrable experience of

leading high\-performing teams, influencing others and delivering value to


customers.

* Experience in SaaS organizations, working with Cloud technologies, AI solutions and Digital Transformation and agile delivery methodologies are desirable.
* Strong leadership, interpersonal skills and presentation proficiency, with excellent written and verbal communication skills, in local language (Italian), Spanish and English
* Proven IT Project Management experience, including budgeting, of complex solutions
* Demonstrated understanding of computer architecture, open systems products (hardware and software), project management tools and methodologies in Customer and/or Professional Services organizations
* Strong management skills including the ability to lead and develop a team of project managers and consultants
* Ability to take the initiative, troubleshoot, build relationships, develop a team, solicit/gain support inside and outside of Professional Services.
* Able to influence thinking or gain acceptance of others during negotiations or sensitive situations
* Proven ability to work under pressure both within a team and on your own.
* Computer literate in the use of MSOffice, MS Project or similar, word processing, spreadsheet, presentation and project control tools.
* Travel regularly (up to 50%) to visit your team and customers within your region, or occasionally to internal company events globally.

 **Desirable**

* Bachelor’s degree or higher in Business, Engineering or Computer Science.
* Project Management Certification, e.g. Prince2, PMI.
* Exposure to Genesys technical solutions. Will have had exposure to the Genesys product suite to a level that you are able to discuss simple solutions.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in Italian and English.
* Experience in sales and delivery of AI solutions
* Ability to work fluently in Italian \& English languages.


If a Genesys employee referred you, please use the link they sent you to apply.

**About Genesys:**


Genesys® empowers more than 8,000 organizations worldwide to create the best customer and employee experiences. With agentic AI at its core, Genesys Cloud™ is the AI\-Powered Experience Orchestration platform that connects people, systems, data and AI across the enterprise. As a result, organizations can drive customer loyalty, growth and retention while increasing operational efficiency and teamwork across human and AI workforces. To learn more, visit www.genesys.com.

**Reasonable Accommodations:**


If you require a reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the application process, or are limited in your ability to access or use this online application and need an alternative method for applying, you or someone you know may contact us at reasonable.accommodations@genesys.com.


You can expect a response within 24–48 hours. To help us provide the best support, click the email link above to open a pre\-filled message and complete the requested information before sending. If you have any questions, please include them in your email.


This email is intended to support job seekers requesting accommodations. Messages unrelated to accommodation—such as application follow\-ups or resume submissions—may not receive a response.

**Genesys is an equal opportunity employer committed to fairness in the workplace. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or** **expression, marital** **status, domestic partner** **status, national** **origin, genetics,** **disability, military** **and veteran status, and other protected characteristics.**

*Please note that recruiters will never ask for sensitive personal or financial information during the application phase.*
