title: Metering Analyst
description:
**Chi è ENGIE?**



Chi è ENGIE: ENGIE mette al centro delle sue attività (elettricità, gas naturale, efficientamento energetico e servizi) lo sviluppo sostenibile, per far fronte alle sfide della transizione energetica verso una low carbon economy: accesso ad un'economia sostenibile, mitigazione e adattamento al cambiamento climatico e ottimizzazione delle risorse.


**Chi cerchiamo?**



Una risorsa da inserire in presso la nostra sede di **Milano (Via Chiese 72\)**, nell’ambito della Global Business Unit **Supply \& Energy Management** \- **One BTC,** , da inserire all’interno della divisione Customer Operations \& Credit Management con il ruolo di **Metering Analyst.**


*ENGIE One B2C è un operatore energetico leader per il mercato residenziale in sei Paesi, con oltre 20 milioni di contratti attivi. Parte della Global Business Unit Supply \& Energy Management, si concentra su energie rinnovabili e servizi energetici per clienti business e consumer. L’obiettivo è aumentare del 60% la distribuzione di energia elettrica e triplicare la quota di energia verde venduta entro il 2030, promuovendo soluzioni innovative e sostenibili.*


**Che cosa farai?**



La risorsa sarà responsabile della gestione end\-to\-end dei processi legati alle misure per le **commodity power e gas**, assicurando la corretta acquisizione, validazione e messa a disposizione dei dati di consumo a supporto dei processi di fatturazione e credit management.



Il ruolo prevede un presidio costante delle performance operative, con forte orientamento al raggiungimento dei KPI di processo e al rispetto delle scadenze regolatorie e operative.



Particolare rilevanza assume il presidio della normativa di settore e delle disposizioni dell’Autorità di Regolazione per Energia Reti e Ambiente (ARERA), con attenzione a:


* corretta gestione dei flussi di misura nel rispetto delle tempistiche regolatorie;
* monitoraggio delle performance connesse agli obblighi di qualità commerciale e tecnica;
* collaborazione con le funzioni Regulatory e Compliance per garantire l’allineamento continuo all’evoluzione normativa.

  


**Quali saranno le tue responsabilità?**


* Monitorare e governare i flussi di misura power e gas, analizzando e risolvendo in modo strutturato le principali cause di scarto.
* Garantire la tempestiva gestione delle anomalie nel rispetto delle deadline di business e delle scadenze regolatorie.
* Supportare Billing nella gestione di casistiche complesse, con particolare attenzione agli impatti economici e regolatori.
* Predisporre reportistica di monitoraggio KPI e analisi di performance, contribuendo all’implementazione di azioni di miglioramento continuo.
* Interagire proattivamente con dipartimenti interni ( IT, Operations, Regulatory ) e stakeholder esterni (prevalentemente fornitori IT), assicurando il rispetto delle segnalazioni effettuate e degli obblighi normativi.


**Perchè tu?**


* Laurea o diploma in discipline tecniche
* Esperienze nell’ambito metering/DSO/billing del settore energetico di almeno 2 anni
* Ottima conoscenza dei principali applicativi di Office Automation (es. Office Word, Excel, Power Point)
* Capacità di lavorare in team anche usando strumenti di condivisione
* Capacità di lavorare per scadenze temporali
* Capacità di analisi dati e presentazione degli stessi


Completano il profilo la conoscenza del Sistema Neta, capacità di operare efficacemente in contesti ad alta complessità e caratterizzati da scadenze stringenti, unita a un solido orientamento al problem solving.


*In ENGIE, ogni talento ha un ruolo fondamentale nell’accelerare la transizione energetica.*


*Contribuisci a fare la differenza e vivi un’esperienza professionale ricca di soddisfazioni.*


*Affronta sfide stimolanti e costruisci un percorso di carriera che rispecchi davvero chi sei.*


*Unisciti a noi e partecipa all’avventura del secolo!*

  


**Perché scegliere ENGIE?**


*Lavorare in ENGIE, gruppo multinazionale, significa avere un'ampia varietà di possibilità di carriera in oltre 30 paesi.*


*Ma significa anche mettere la comunità al centro della tua vita professionale e contribuire a un vero cambiamento nel corso di una carriera stimolante.*


*Il nostro impegno è chiaro: sosteniamo un modello sociale che concilia la performance economica con l'impatto positivo sulle persone e sul pianeta. Scommettiamo sul potere della collettività e agiamo come parte di una comunità impegnata a sconvolgere e reinventare il futuro. Diversità e inclusione sono al centro della nostra cultura aziendale.*


*ENGIE si impegna a combattere tutte le forme di discriminazione in materia di occupazione, accesso alla formazione e promozione. Pertanto, l'integrazione e il sostegno di giovani, adulti, persone con disabilità, senza alcuna distinzione di genere, etnia o religione, sono priorità fondamentali della politica delle risorse umane.* *Riconosciuti per il 2025 dal Sole24Ore come azienda leader in diversità \& inclusione, le certificazioni ISO 30415:2021 e UNI PDR 125:2022 sono l’ulteriore dimostrazione del nostro impegno.*

  


**Business Unit:** Supply \& Energy Management
**Division:** One B2C \- Italy
**Legal Entity:** ENGIE ITALIA S.P.A
**Professional Experience:** Junior (experience \< 3 years)
**Education Level:** Technical College Diploma
**Company Name:** ENGIE Italia

title: Tech Lead - Mobile Platform (Remote, Full-Time) [AS227] Europe
description:
**About Smart Working**



At Smart Working, we believe your job should not only look right on paper but also feel right every day. This isn’t just another remote opportunity \- it’s about finding where you truly belong, no matter where you are. From day one, you’re welcomed into a genuine community that values your growth and well\-being.



Our mission is simple: to break down geographic barriers and connect skilled professionals with outstanding global teams and products for full\-time, long\-term roles. We help you discover meaningful work with teams that invest in your success, where you’re empowered to grow personally and professionally.  

Join one of the highest\-rated workplaces on Glassdoor and experience what it means to thrive in a truly remote\-first world.


**About the Role**



We are looking for a **Tech Lead \- Mobile Platform**to own and strengthen the technical foundations of our mobile banking app. This is a platform\-focused leadership role, not a product feature lead or purely hands\-on mobile IC position, with a strong emphasis on platform enablement, developer experience, engineering architecture, release quality, and mobile standards. The role supports a small mobile team of approximately 3 engineers working primarily with React Native across iOS and Android.

  

  

The primary purpose of this role is to ensure that the mobile platform operates as a reliable, high\-quality foundation so that product teams can move faster with confidence. This means understanding the product teams' needs, removing blockers, driving adoption of new tools and processes through mentoring, documentation and training, and setting high engineering standards for the mobile codebase, while carefully managing the trade\-off between standards and product team flow.  

  

While the role is primarily leadership\-focused, it requires sufficient hands\-on depth to guide architectural decisions and platform improvements. The team currently has native libraries written in Kotlin, Swift, Objective\-C, and Java that require ongoing maintenance and support. There is also a future consideration around a potential move to Expo, which would be explored and decided collaboratively with the new hire.


### **Responsibilities**

* Lead, mentor, and provide technical direction to the mobile platform team, with a focus on enabling product teams to operate as efficiently as possible through clear prioritisation of platform initiatives.
* Define, implement, and uphold engineering standards across mobile security, observability, performance, resilience, and release quality, continuously balancing standards improvement with minimal impact on product team velocity.
* Act as a hands\-on technical leader in the development and maintenance of shared mobile platform capabilities within React Native and TypeScript.
* Contribute to architectural decisions relating to React Native, native modules, and integrations across Kotlin, Swift, Objective\-C, and Java.
* Own and drive improvements to CI/CD pipelines, build reliability, release workflows, and deployment processes, a critical and essential responsibility for this role.
* Own mobile platform\-level observability and crash reporting, including tooling for logging, analytics, exception monitoring, and production stability; general service alerting is managed by product domain teams.
* Partner with product feature teams to ensure releases are delivered smoothly, meet quality expectations, and align with regulatory and compliance requirements.
* Drive improvements in automated testing practices, including unit, integration, and end\-to\-end testing approaches that improve confidence in release quality.
* Own and coordinate production incident management and release activities, ensuring timely resolution, clear communication, and strong operational control.
* Collaborate with design and product teams to evolve and maintain shared UI components, platform standards, and mobile design system capabilities.
* Drive adoption of new tools, processes, and standards across product teams through mentoring, documentation, and training, ensuring improvements are embedded in practice, not just defined on paper.
* Identify and drive opportunities to improve platform stability, developer productivity, and the long\-term maintainability of the mobile application estate.
* Promote secure application development practices and support compliance\-aligned delivery within a regulated banking environment.
* Evaluate tooling evolution where relevant, including potential future considerations such as a move to Expo, in close collaboration with the team.

### **Requirements**

* 8\+ years of experience in mobile engineering, including significant time operating at a Tech Lead, Mobile Platform Lead, Staff, or Principal Engineer level.
* 4\+ years of production experience building and maintaining mobile applications with React Native and TypeScript, this is the core of the stack.
* 4\+ years of experience with native mobile development (Kotlin, Swift, Objective\-C, or Java), required for maintaining and supporting native libraries already in use.
* Own and drive improvements to CI/CD pipelines, build reliability, release processes, deployment workflows, and broader developer workflows, this is an essential requirement for the role.
* Strong understanding of mobile architecture, including React Native bridges, native modules, and platform\-level architectural decisions.
* Experience owning or significantly improving mobile platform observability, crash reporting (e.g. Firebase Crashlytics, Sentry, Datadog), exception monitoring, and production incident handling.
* Strong experience with mobile testing approaches, including unit, integration, and end\-to\-end testing (e.g. Detox, Appium, Jest) to drive release confidence.
* Solid understanding of mobile engineering standards across performance, resilience, reliability, and quality.
* 1\+ year of experience leading a mobile platform team, with demonstrated responsibility for technical direction, mentoring, and engineering standards.
* Track record of understanding product team needs and prioritising platform work to remove blockers and accelerate product delivery.
* Demonstrated ability to drive adoption of new tools and processes across teams, through documentation, training, and hands\-on mentoring, not just defining standards.
* Experience setting and maintaining a high\-quality bar for a mobile codebase while managing the ongoing trade\-off between standards and team velocity.
* Strong cross\-functional collaboration skills across engineering, product, design, and where relevant, compliance and regulatory stakeholders.
* Prior experience leading at least one significant technical or platform project end\-to\-end.
* Experience managing or directly leading engineers, with a people\-first approach to technical leadership.

### **Nice to Have**

* Prior experience in a fintech, banking, or regulated product environment
* Stronger depth in native mobile development (Kotlin, Swift, Objective\-C, or Java) beyond integration and maintenance support.
* Experience evaluating or supporting React Native tooling evolution, including Expo or similar migration considerations.
* Exposure to shared platform components, design systems, or reusable mobile UI library development and maintenance.
* Experience with secure mobile development practices or a background that includes mobile security\-focused work.
* Familiarity with mobile performance optimisation, resilience patterns, or broader non\-functional quality standards.


At Smart Working, you’ll never be just another remote hire.



Be a Smart Worker \- valued, empowered, and part of a culture that celebrates integrity, excellence, and ambition.



If that sounds like your kind of place, we’d love to hear your story.



We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Managing Director - MRS-GM Italy (m/f/d*)
description:
**Location** Milan, Italy **Job type** Full\-Time **Work mode** Hybrid **Job level** Management \& Leadership **Job ID** 12519 **Company** Munich Re Specialty \- Global Markets **Employment type** Regular **Area of expertise** Client \& Customer Management
  
**About Us**
At Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets (MRS\-GM), it is our ambition to become the leading Primary Specialty Insurance provider, underpinned by an effective and adaptable strategy, superior products, and industry leaders working in a supportive environment to achieve this.
At the heart of our success is a strong culture where people are encouraged to be present, bold and curious, allowing them to achieve their individual goals.*Please see our website for more information.***Managing Director, MRS\-GM Italy**
Munich Re Specialty was formed as a stand\-alone division in 2023, bringing together the wider Munich Re Group’s primary specialty insurance businesses. Building on strong foundations in the UK and the US, **Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets (MRS****‑****GM)** is executing an ambitious strategy to expand its footprint across key European markets.
As part of this growth journey, we are seeking a Managing Director, MRS‑GM Italy, to establish, lead, and grow our presence in the Italian market.
This is a senior, market‑facing leadership role with full accountability for defining and executing the Italian business strategy, building local underwriting and distribution capabilities, and establishing Munich Re Specialty’s Italian operations from the ground up. As **Managing Director**, you will define and execute the local strategy, build a high‑performing team, and act as the face and voice of Munich Re Specialty in Italy.
Working in close collaboration with senior leaders across Europe and the wider Munich Re Group, you will combine entrepreneurial drive with disciplined underwriting and operational excellence to deliver sustainable and profitable growth, while upholding Munich Re’s reputation for integrity and quality.
This role is ideally suited to an influential, market‑facing leader who enjoys building businesses, developing teams, and shaping strategy within a global specialty insurance environment.
The role reports to the CEO, MRS‑GM Europe.**Responsibilities****Strategic \& Business Leadership*** Create and execute a comprehensive, scalable, and sustainable strategy for the Italian market, aligned with MRS‑GM’s European and global objectives.
* Establish Munich Re Specialty’s presence in Italy, growing the business to EUR 100m\+ within five years with a focus on the middle market.
* Implement an efficient and scalable operating model that supports long‑term growth and future European expansion.
* Act as the face and voice of MRS‑GM in Italy, embodying and representing the Munich Re Specialty brand in the market.

**Market Development \& Client Engagement*** Develop and maintain long‑term relationships with global, regional, and local brokers, MGAs, and key clients to drive new business and strengthen existing partnerships.
* Actively monitor market developments, competitor activity, and emerging opportunities to enhance MRS‑GM’s market positioning.
* Proactively engage with internal stakeholders locally and internationally (including London and Munich), as well as Reinsurance, Facultative \& Corporate and Great Lakes, to ensure underwriting and business best practices are applied.

**Leadership \& People Development*** Recruit, develop, and lead a local Italian team from the ground up (‘boots on the ground’), starting with underwriting capabilities.
* Set clear objectives and support staff development through continuous performance conversations, coaching, and knowledge sharing.
* Ensure that all team members meet operational, regulatory, and compliance requirements.

**Governance, Performance \& Risk Management*** Ensure that underwriting, distribution, and operational activities align with the Italian business plan and global underwriting strategy.
* Oversee portfolio performance, ensuring disciplined underwriting, regulatory compliance, and sustainable profitability, working in close partnership with Great Lakes Italy to support adherence to governance and regulatory standards.
* Conduct peer reviews and ensure adherence to delegated authorities, company procedures, and compliance standards.
* Produce management reports and insights as required to support decision‑making and performance tracking.
* Ensure adherence to delegated authorities, company procedures, and compliance standards through peer review and disciplined oversight.

**Knowledge and Skills*** Extensive, proven experience as a senior business leader within the primary insurance market in Italy, with a strong track record of delivering profitable growth.
* Highly regarded and well‑connected within the Italian insurance market, with an established portfolio of senior broker relationships.
* Strong understanding of operating within a regulated financial services environment, with the ability to act as an approved senior person in the role.
* Entrepreneurial, resilient, and comfortable building operations in an evolving or greenfield setup.
* Strategic thinker with the ability to articulate and execute ambitious business plans while inspiring teams to deliver results.
* Strong commercial judgment, solution‑oriented mindset, and ability to balance innovation with governance.
* Credibility at senior stakeholder level, underpinned by integrity, professionalism, and sound decision‑making.
* International mindset with strong people leadership, change management, and emotional intelligence.
* Fluent in Italian and English, written and spoken.

*If you are motivated by the opportunity to establish and lead Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets in Italy, we would be delighted to hear from you.***Diversity, Equity \& Inclusion**
At Munich Re, Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion foster innovation and resilience and enable us to act braver and better. Embracing the power of DEI is at the core of who we are. We recognise diversity can be multi\-dimensional, intersectional, and complex, so we want to build a diverse workforce that includes a wide range of racial, ethnic, sexual, and gender identities; economic and geographic backgrounds; physical abilities; ages; life, school, and career experiences; and political, religious, and personal beliefs. Additionally, we are committed to building an equitable and inclusive work environment where this diversity is celebrated, valued, and has equitable opportunities to succeed.
All candidates in consideration for any role can request a reasonable adjustment at any point in our recruitment process. You can request an adjustment by speaking to your Talent Acquisition contact.**Learning and innovating today, striving for sustainable societies and business tomorrow**
At Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets our approach to ESG is underpinned by our desire to seize business opportunities and to nurture a stimulating and inclusive work environment. Our ESG strategy aims to deliver holistic impacts across environmental, social and governance topics including supporting a number of local initiatives within our community and offering volunteering opportunities for colleagues

title: Team Project Coordinator
description:
Want a 3D Career? Join Norgine.


At Norgine, our colleagues Dare themselves to be different and try new things, Drive to achieve their goals and beyond, and Develop themselves and their community.


We call it the 3D career at Norgine and it offers you a fully\-rounded experience with no limits.


Bring everything about yourself that you’re proud of, whether that’s your passion for making a difference, focus on others’ well\-being, or intellectual curiosity to unleash in a fast\-paced environment and supportive community.


In return, get a sense of belonging, a long\-term career with ongoing development and upskilling, and a company that cares about people’s wellness as much as you do.


Because at Norgine, we transform lives with innovative healthcare solutions.


We have an exciting opportunity for a Team Project Coordinator to join Norgine.


* Reports to the General Manager (with a dotted line to the HR Manager for matters relating to personnel administration)
* Direct management of two resources


If you want a multi\-dimensional 3D career in a leading healthcare organisation, join us.  



  

  

**Requirements**  

Organisational Coordination \& People Leadership

* Coordinate the team of assistants, assigning tasks, balancing workloads and monitoring deadlines, quality and priorities. The team of assistants will handle activities involving internal and external stakeholders, as well as staff administrative tasks
* Act as the operational point of contact for all departments within ROC Italy.
* Fulfil a dual role: supervising and directly managing activities both personally and through the team, according to operational needs.
* Monitor and ensure that the team bases its activities on processes, templates and operational SOPs, guaranteeing standardisation and continuous improvement.
* Oversee the schedule, deadlines and deliverables of cross\-functional projects with regular reporting.


The responsibilities and activities of the Team Project Coordinator and the team of assistants include, but are not limited to, the following:


Activities towards internal stakeholders:


Manage all office\-related activities, including the management and preparation of meeting rooms, the organisation of internal meetings (including support in organising national meetings), and organisational coordination with global and external stakeholders.


Internal communication


Foster alignment and engagement among all colleagues through updates and digital channels – intranet, newsletters, town hall meetings, and disease\-specific awareness days


Conferences and educational events – Supervision and operations

* Overseeing conferences and educational events (ECM/non\-ECM) related activities performed by Norgine external congress management partner: liaising with secretariats, AIFA checks and updating congress management systems.
* Direct management of purchase orders and uploads to operational systems.
* Management of travel and hospitality for guests, including selection and negotiation with agencies.
* Support for Farmindustria certification and attendance at audits where required.


Activities involving external stakeholders (supervision \& operations)

* Supervision and support for the organisation of focus groups, market research, advisory boards, consultancy and sponsorship.
* End\-to\-end management of contractual processes: liaising with suppliers/associations/professionals, preparing documentation, collecting signatures and archiving.
* Purchase order management: supplier onboarding, issuance and follow\-up, checks and budget file management.
* Tracking grant requests and liaising with HCPs and scientific societies regarding the contacts necessary to complete the administrative and contractual process.


External communication


Supporting the visibility of the company’s image to external stakeholders – e.g. Norgine Italia website, LinkedIn, etc.


Promotional Materials \& Logistics – Operational Supervision

* Management of shipments and requirements for conference stands.
* Dispatching materials to the sales force.
* Support with the approval process for promotional materials.


Supervision of promotional material stock reconciliation with DHL


Local IT Point – Operational supervision

* Liaising with Global IT regarding PC fleet management and other IT support


Management of relations with external consultants.


Personnel administration (supervision) – in line with HR

* Company car fleet (supervision of external supplier)
* General HR administration


Compliance \& Audit (support to BI\&CM)

* Supporting the operational, documentary and organisational management of compliance processes, facilitating the correct application of internal policies, codes and industry guidelines


Reporting, KPIs \& Continuous Improvement (supervision \& operations)

* Updating the events pipeline, budget vs actual, and supplier KPIs.
* Identifying opportunities for process optimisation and digitalisation.


Role requirements


Education \& Experience


Proven seniority in project/operations coordination within pharmaceutical companies.

* Proven experience in sales support and/or conference management within the pharmaceutical sector.
* Knowledge of codes and regulations within the pharmaceutical sector


Soft skills

* Managerial skills in team management and workload management.
* Collaborative leadership and the ability to create a positive working environment.
* Project management, prioritisation and a results\-oriented approach.
* Problem\-solving and the ability to manage multiple projects in dynamic environments.
* Clear communication, negotiation and excellent customer service.
* Discretion and the ability to interact with all levels of the organisation.
* Confidentiality in handling information

 **Benefits**  

Our benefits may vary per location. Please liaise with the Norgine TA representative to obtain more information.


Sound good? Find out more about the career you’ll have with Norgine, then apply here.

  

\#LI\-PP1
